stage.subtitle_ar: محطّتكِ الأخيرة: تقرئين الأخبار والتوثيق التقني بالإنجليزية، وتتحدّثين عن التقنية برأيٍ واضحٍ وواثق.

content.intro_ar: التقنية تتحرّك كلّ يوم، ومهندسة المستقبل تتابع مجالها من مصادره الأصلية بالإنجليزية. في هذا الدرس ستتعلّمين كيف تقرئين خبرًا تقنيًّا بذكاء: تفهمين العنوان، وتقيّمين المصدر، وتميّزين الحقيقة من الإشاعة. هذه المهارة ستجعل قراءتكِ أسرع وثقتكِ أكبر.

vocab.meaning_en: the title of a news story, in large letters
vocab.example: The headline said a new phone was coming.

vocab.meaning_en: a piece of writing about one topic
vocab.example: I read a long article about artificial intelligence.

vocab.meaning_en: the place where information comes from
vocab.example: Always check the source of the news.

vocab.meaning_en: a newer version with changes or fixes
vocab.example: The company released a security update.

vocab.meaning_en: when a product is made available to people
vocab.example: The release of the new app is next week.

vocab.meaning_en: something a product can do
vocab.example: My favourite feature is the dark mode.

vocab.meaning_en: to tell people about something officially
vocab.example: They will announce the price tomorrow.

vocab.meaning_en: a story that people share but may not be true
vocab.example: That is just a rumor, not a fact.

vocab.meaning_en: something you can trust to be correct
vocab.example: This website is a reliable source.

vocab.meaning_en: something that is popular right now
vocab.example: Cloud computing is a big trend this year.

vocab.meaning_en: a short version that gives the main points
vocab.example: Read the summary before the full text.

vocab.meaning_en: to say something is true without full proof
vocab.example: They claim it is the fastest laptop ever.

paragraphs: Every day, thousands of tech articles appear online. A good headline tells you the main idea in a few words, but it can also be a trick to make you click. Smart readers do not stop at the headline; they read the full article and think about the source. Is the website reliable, or does it just repeat a rumor?

Tech news often talks about a new release. A company may announce an update to its software or launch a phone with a new feature. Reporters explain what changed and why it matters. When a headline says a product is 'the best ever,' ask for proof. A reliable article shows real numbers and quotes experts, not just a claim from an anonymous person.

To save time, many readers start with a short summary at the top of the page. This gives the key points before the details. It also helps to follow a current trend, so you understand what people in tech are talking about this week. However, one source is never enough. If two or three reliable websites report the same news, you can trust it more.
Reading tech news in English is great practice. You learn new words, you follow your future field, and you slowly build the confidence to tell a fact from a rumor.

key_phrases.en: According to a reliable source, the update is free.
The company announced a new feature today.
key_phrases.ar: أعلنت الشركة عن ميزةٍ جديدةٍ اليوم.

key_phrases.en: This is just a rumor, not a confirmed fact.
key_phrases.ar: هذه مجرّد إشاعة، وليست حقيقةً مؤكّدة.

key_phrases.en: Let me read the full article before I decide.
key_phrases.ar: دعيني أقرأ المقال كاملًا قبل أن أقرّر.

key_phrases.en: What is the source of this news?

comprehension.question: What does a good headline do?
options: Gives the main idea in a few words
Writes the whole article for you
comprehension.correct: Gives the main idea in a few words
comprehension.explanation_ar: العنوان الجيّد يلخّص الفكرة الرئيسية بكلماتٍ قليلة.

comprehension.question: Why should you not trust the headline alone?
options: Because it can be a trick to make you click
Because it is too long to read
comprehension.correct: Because it can be a trick to make you click
comprehension.explanation_ar: أحيانًا يكون العنوان مصمّمًا لإغرائكِ بالنقر فقط، فاقرئي المقال كاملًا.

comprehension.question: What makes an article more reliable?
options: Real numbers and quotes from experts
comprehension.correct: Real numbers and quotes from experts
comprehension.explanation_ar: المقال الموثوق يقدّم أرقامًا حقيقية ويقتبس من خبراء، لا مجرّد ادّعاء.

comprehension.question: According to the text, what helps you save time?
options: Reading a hundred articles at once
comprehension.explanation_ar: البدء بملخّصٍ قصير يعطيكِ النقاط الأساسية بسرعة.

comprehension.question: When can you trust a piece of news more?
options: When several reliable websites report the same news
When only one person claims it
When it has the biggest headline
When it is a popular rumor
comprehension.correct: When several reliable websites report the same news
comprehension.explanation_ar: تأكيد الخبر من عدّة مصادر موثوقة يزيد من ثقتكِ به.

task.prompt_en: Find one short tech headline online. Write 3–4 sentences: what it says, who the source is, and whether you think it is reliable.
task.prompt_ar: ابحثي عن عنوانٍ تقنيٍّ قصير على الإنترنت، ثم اكتبي ٣–٤ جُمل: ماذا يقول، ومن هو مصدره، وهل تظنّينه موثوقًا.
task.guidance_ar: استخدمي ٣ كلمات على الأقل من كلمات الدرس، مثل: source، reliable، rumor، update.

content.intro_ar: لا يحفظ أيّ مبرمجٍ كلّ شيء؛ لهذا يعود المحترفون دائمًا إلى «التوثيق» (the docs). في هذا الدرس ستتعلّمين كيف تقرئين صفحة توثيقٍ بالإنجليزية بثقة: تفهمين الدالة ومعاملاتها والقيمة التي تُرجِعها، وتلتقطين التحذيرات مثل «مهجور» و«القيمة الافتراضية». إتقان هذه القراءة سيوفّر عليكِ ساعاتٍ من العمل.

vocab.meaning_en: the official written text that explains how a tool works
vocab.example: The documentation explains every function.

vocab.meaning_en: a detailed part of the docs you look things up in
vocab.example: Check the reference for the exact name.

vocab.meaning_en: a value you give to a function so it can work
vocab.example: This function takes two parameters.

vocab.meaning_en: a named piece of code that does one job
vocab.example: I wrote a function to add two numbers.

vocab.meaning_en: the correct way to write the code
vocab.example: A small syntax error can break the program.

vocab.meaning_en: a short piece of real code that shows how to use something
vocab.example: Copy the example and run it.

vocab.meaning_en: to add a program or library to your system
vocab.example: First, install the library.

vocab.meaning_en: a text that teaches you step by step
vocab.example: The 'Getting Started' guide is very clear.

vocab.meaning_en: the value a function gives back after it runs
vocab.example: This function returns the total.

vocab.meaning_en: no longer recommended and may be removed later
vocab.example: That method is deprecated now.

vocab.meaning_en: the value used automatically if you choose nothing
vocab.example: The default color is blue.

vocab.meaning_en: one part of a longer document
vocab.example: The examples are in this section.

paragraphs: Every serious tool, library, or programming language comes with documentation, often called 'the docs.' Developers rarely remember everything, so they read the docs to learn how something works. Good documentation is well organized: it usually starts with a 'Getting Started' guide, then a detailed reference for every function. Learning to read it quickly is one of the most valuable skills in tech.

A typical reference page describes one function at a time. It tells you the name, the parameters it accepts, and the value it will return. It also shows the correct syntax, so you know exactly how to write the code. Most pages include a short example, because seeing real code is often clearer than a long explanation. When you are stuck, copy the example, run it, and change it step by step.

Documentation also warns you about problems. If a feature is deprecated, it means the developers no longer recommend it and may remove it in the future, so you should avoid it in new projects. The docs also mention the default value of each parameter — the value the tool uses when you do not choose one yourself. Reading these details carefully saves hours of debugging.

Before you install a new library, it is wise to skim the whole documentation. Read the main sections, check the requirements, and look for a quick example. In English, the docs use the same words again and again, so the more you read, the faster and more confident you become.

key_phrases.en: Let me check the documentation first.
This function takes two parameters and returns a value.
key_phrases.ar: هذه الدالة تأخذ معاملَين وتُرجِع قيمة.

key_phrases.en: What is the default value of this parameter?
This method is deprecated; use the new one instead.
key_phrases.ar: هذه الطريقة مهجورة؛ استخدمي الطريقة الجديدة بدلًا منها.

key_phrases.en: There is a helpful example in this section.
key_phrases.ar: يوجد مثالٌ مفيد في هذا القسم.

comprehension.question: What is 'the docs' short for?
comprehension.explanation_ar: «the docs» اختصارٌ شائع لكلمة documentation، أي التوثيق.

comprehension.question: What does a reference page usually describe?
options: One function, its parameters, and its return value
comprehension.correct: One function, its parameters, and its return value
comprehension.explanation_ar: صفحة المرجع تصف دالةً واحدة: اسمها، ومعاملاتها، والقيمة التي تُرجِعها.

comprehension.question: What does 'deprecated' mean?
options: It is recommended for new projects
The developers no longer recommend it and may remove it
comprehension.correct: The developers no longer recommend it and may remove it
comprehension.explanation_ar: «deprecated» تعني مهجور: لم يعد يُنصح به وقد يُحذف مستقبلًا.

comprehension.question: What is a 'default value'?
options: The value the tool uses when you do not choose one
A value you must always type
comprehension.correct: The value the tool uses when you do not choose one
comprehension.explanation_ar: القيمة الافتراضية هي ما تستخدمه الأداة تلقائيًّا عندما لا تختارين قيمة.

comprehension.question: Why do many pages include an example?
options: Because seeing real code is often clearer than a long explanation
comprehension.correct: Because seeing real code is often clearer than a long explanation
comprehension.explanation_ar: رؤية كودٍ حقيقي غالبًا أوضح من شرحٍ طويل، لذلك تضع الصفحات مثالًا.

comprehension.question: What is wise to do before you install a new library?
comprehension.explanation_ar: من الحكمة تصفّح التوثيق كاملًا بسرعة قبل تثبيت أيّ مكتبةٍ جديدة.

task.prompt_en: Choose one simple function you know (or imagine one). Write a short documentation entry: its name, its parameters, what it returns, and one example line.
task.prompt_ar: اختاري دالةً بسيطةً تعرفينها (أو تخيّلي واحدة)، ثم اكتبي مدخلًا مختصرًا في التوثيق: اسمها، ومعاملاتها، وما الذي تُرجِعه، وسطرَ مثالٍ واحد.
task.guidance_ar: استخدمي كلماتٍ مثل: function، parameter، return، example، default.

content.intro_ar: قراءة التقنية نصف المهارة؛ نصفها الآخر أن تتحدّثي عنها. في الصف أو العمل أو المقابلة، سيُطلب منكِ أن تشرحي رأيكِ وتقارني بين الأدوات وتوصي بحلّ. في هذا الدرس ستتعلّمين عباراتٍ إنجليزية تجعل حديثكِ التقني واضحًا ومحترمًا وواثقًا، مبنيًّا على أسبابٍ لا على كلماتٍ صعبة.

vocab.meaning_en: what you think about something
vocab.example: In my opinion, this tool is better.

vocab.meaning_en: to make an idea clear to someone
vocab.example: Can you explain how it works?

vocab.meaning_en: to look at two things and show how they differ
vocab.example: Let us compare the two frameworks.

vocab.meaning_en: a good point about something
vocab.example: One advantage is its speed.

vocab.meaning_en: a weak point or disadvantage
vocab.example: The main drawback is the price.

vocab.meaning_en: to suggest something as a good choice
vocab.example: I recommend this library to beginners.

vocab.meaning_en: something you can depend on
vocab.example: This server is very reliable.

vocab.meaning_en: getting good results without wasting time or power
vocab.example: The new code is more efficient.

vocab.meaning_en: a problem that needs attention
vocab.example: We found an issue in the login page.

vocab.meaning_en: a way to fix a problem
vocab.example: I have a simple solution for that.

vocab.meaning_en: to have the same opinion as someone
vocab.example: I agree with your idea.

vocab.meaning_en: when you gain one thing but lose another
vocab.example: Speed and cost are a trade-off.

paragraphs: Learning to read tech content is only half of the skill; you also need to talk about it. In class, at work, or in an interview, you will be asked to share your opinion and to explain your ideas clearly. People will not simply want to know what a tool does — they will want to know what you think of it and why.

A common task is to compare two technologies. When you compare, you describe the advantages and the drawbacks of each one. For example, one framework may be very fast and efficient, while another is easier to learn but a little slower. Almost every real choice in tech is a trade-off, and a good engineer can explain that balance in plain English instead of hiding behind difficult words.

When someone reports an issue, the discussion moves toward a solution. You might say, 'I recommend this library because it is reliable and well documented.' Others may agree with you, or they may disagree politely and suggest a different approach. Good technical conversation is respectful: you give reasons, you listen, and you accept a better idea when you hear one.

To sound natural, learn a few useful patterns. Phrases like 'In my opinion,' 'One advantage is,' and 'The main drawback is' help you organize your thoughts. The goal is not to memorize hard words, but to speak with confidence and to make your reasoning easy for others to follow.

key_phrases.en: In my opinion, this is the better choice.
key_phrases.ar: في رأيي، هذا هو الخيار الأفضل.

key_phrases.en: One advantage is speed; the main drawback is cost.
key_phrases.ar: من مزاياه السرعة، وأبرز عيوبه التكلفة.

key_phrases.en: I recommend this tool because it is reliable.
I agree with you, but I would add one point.
key_phrases.ar: أتّفق معكِ، لكنّني أضيف نقطةً واحدة.

key_phrases.en: Let me explain the issue and suggest a solution.

comprehension.question: According to the text, reading tech content is:
comprehension.explanation_ar: القراءة نصف المهارة فقط؛ نصفها الآخر أن تتحدّثي عن التقنية.

comprehension.question: What do you do when you 'compare' two technologies?
options: You describe the advantages and drawbacks of each
comprehension.correct: You describe the advantages and drawbacks of each
comprehension.explanation_ar: المقارنة تعني ذكر مزايا وعيوب كلّ خيار.

comprehension.question: What is a 'trade-off'?
options: A tool that has no drawbacks
A perfect solution with no cost
A balance where you gain something and lose something
comprehension.correct: A balance where you gain something and lose something
comprehension.explanation_ar: المقايضة (trade-off) هي موازنة: تكسبين شيئًا وتخسرين آخر.

comprehension.question: According to the text, good technical conversation is:
options: Respectful — you give reasons and listen
comprehension.correct: Respectful — you give reasons and listen
comprehension.explanation_ar: الحوار التقني الجيّد محترم: تقدّمين الأسباب، وتستمعين، وتقبلين الفكرة الأفضل.

comprehension.question: Which phrase helps you share your view?
comprehension.explanation_ar: عبارة «In my opinion» تمهّد لعرض رأيكِ بوضوح.

comprehension.question: What is the real goal, according to the last paragraph?
options: To speak with confidence and clear reasoning
To talk as fast as possible
comprehension.correct: To speak with confidence and clear reasoning
comprehension.explanation_ar: الهدف ليس حفظ الكلمات الصعبة، بل التحدّث بثقة وبأسبابٍ واضحة.

task.prompt_en: Choose two apps or tools you use. Speak for one minute: compare them, give one advantage and one drawback of each, and say which you recommend and why.
task.prompt_ar: اختاري تطبيقَين أو أداتَين تستخدمينهما، ثم تحدّثي لمدّة دقيقة: قارني بينهما، واذكري ميزةً وعيبًا لكلٍّ منهما، وقولي أيّهما توصين به ولماذا.
task.guidance_ar: استخدمي عباراتٍ مثل: In my opinion، One advantage is، The main drawback is، I recommend.
